crop_context: major food crops (wheat rice maize soybean)
stress_signal: yield decline under warming and altered water availability
yield_or_biomass_effect: Projected yield losses vary by crop; C3 and C4 crops respond differently to heat drought and CO2 interactions
notes: Good anchor for why stress profiles must remain crop-specific.

crop_context: multiple crops under drought heat and salinity
stress_type: leaf damage photosynthesis decline morphological reduction
stress_signal: flowering and grain-filling are especially sensitive
growth_stage_context: yield and developmental impairment under stress
priority_score: Useful broad stress framework for signal and stage ranking.

stress_type: drought compaction N and P deficiency waterlogging
stress_signal: root morphological physiological and anatomical responses
growth_stage_context: stress-specific across growth and root development phases
yield_or_biomass_effect: Root growth impairment is tightly linked to wheat stress vulnerability and yield loss
support_logic: root architecture and associated management strategies are central support factors
notes: High-priority source for ranking root traits as resilience supports.

crop_context: multiple crops under drought and salinity
stress_type: multi-trait visual physiological and remote-sensed indicators
growth_stage_context: stress tolerance differs by crop and trait
support_factor: one-signal interpretation is too weak for comparative resilience work
priority_score: Useful for the stress panel and cross-crop comparison logic.

crop_context: multiple crops under diverse abiotic stresses
stress_type: visual physiological biochemical and molecular indicators
stress_signal: variable across stress stage and crop
growth_stage_context: stress affects biomass production and later yield
support_factor: stress should be profiled across indicator classes not only visual symptoms
priority_score: Useful as taxonomy for the signal layer rather than as direct field validation.

crop_context: multiple crops in resilient production systems
stress_type: drought and pest-related stress buffering through silicon support
growth_stage_context: supports more sustainable and resilient crop performance
support_factor: silicon should be treated as a serious support-factor candidate rather than a niche amendment
priority_score: Important for keeping the support-factor shortlist broader than only microbial lines.
